seoTitle: North Tammin vs North Walpole — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between North Tammin and North Walpole. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: North Tammin vs North Walpole: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Tammin and North Walpole.
heading: North Tammin vs North Walpole — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Tammin, Western Australia and North Walpole, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Walpole has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to North Tammin (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Tammin or North Walpole safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Tammin has a flood risk score of 53/100, while North Walpole scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Tammin scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. North Walpole scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Tammin or North Walpole?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Walpole has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
